payload.message: A useful architecture detail is that KV cache latency wins depend on cache layout, not just on the abstract idea of reuse. If key and value tensors are paged poorly across devices or batches, decode latency can still spike because memory movement dominates the saved compute. Strong serving designs pair cache reuse with paging policy, admission control, and batching rules so the latency benefit survives under realistic traffic rather than only in clean offline benchmarks.
